nodes.label: Feedback Alignment & DFA
nodes.rationale: Replace W^T with a fixed random B; forward weights self-align so the random signal becomes a descent direction (<90 degrees). Kills weight transport, de-risks the no-W^T stance; but fails at ImageNet scale, which argues for structured attribution over random feedback.

nodes.label: Weight-transport problem
nodes.rationale: Backprop's backward path needs W^T of every forward weight, kept synced; long treated as the decisive reason backprop can't be physical. The single most-cited objection FA/DFA answer.
